11.title: RENUKAMATA 28/5/2025
11.cta: DM to plan your space right

15.title: make my mandir
15.caption:
Before you DM us, here are the 5 questions we get asked most.

#CustomMandir #MandirFAQ #MakeMyMandir #PuneInteriors

brandDNA.archetype: CAREGIVER blended with SAGE. We nurture the client through a sacred decision (Caregiver) while bringing deep expertise in materials, design, and Vastu (Sage). Not a Magician (no big transformation drama) and not a Ruler (no premium snobbery).
brandDNA.personality_traits:
Warm. Devotional. Patient. Knowledgeable. Honest. Family-oriented.

MakeMyMandir is the kind of person who treats your mandir like it's going into their own home — they'd rather slow down and design it right than rush a sale.
brandDNA.personality_donts:
NOT a furniture brand — we make sacred spaces, not cupboards.

NOT a discount retailer — we don't compete on price; we compete on design and material integrity.

NOT pushy — we don't chase signatures, we let the design do the convincing.

NOT generic — every quote is preceded by a design.

NOT trendy — we don't follow fads. Mandirs are forever.
brandDNA.brand_age_metaphor: A senior craftsman-designer in their late 40s — experienced enough to know every material's quirk, modern enough to use 3D software, devout enough to take the work seriously. Treats clients like family, not transactions.
brandDNA.primary_persona:
Name: Pradeep & Anjali Sharma
Age: 38-50
Family: Married couple with 1-2 children; parents may live with them
Work: Salaried professional (engineer / manager / doctor) OR self-employed business owner
Income: ₹15L–₹50L+ household annual
Location: Pune (primary) — also Mumbai, Nashik, Aurangabad, Kolhapur; NRIs gifting parents

Life moment: Renovating their home, moving into a new flat, or upgrading from a basic wood mandir they bought 10 years ago.

Values: Tradition + modern living together. Their home reflects them; their mandir should too. Devout but not orthodox. Educated, comparing options, willing to invest in quality.

Googles: "custom mandir Pune", "corian mandir vs wood", "mandir vastu direction", "pooja room design ideas".

Fears: Spending ₹50k–₹2L on something that looks wrong in their home. Paying for design and not liking the result. Choosing the wrong material and regretting it 5 years later. Vastu mistakes.
brandDNA.primary_persona_quote: "We didn't want one of those ready-made mandirs from the showroom — they all look the same. We wanted a mandir that fits OUR home, our idols, our budget. MakeMyMandir actually designed it for us before we paid anything. That made all the difference."[Nilesh — replace this with an actual quote from a real client if possible.]
brandDNA.secondary_personas:
INTERIOR DESIGNER (Pune/Mumbai): Working on a luxury residential project, needs a custom mandir that matches their overall interior design language. Specs sent in CAD-compatible formats, needs designer-grade rendering and on-time delivery.

NRI GIFTING PARENTS: Lives in US/UK/Gulf, wants to gift parents in India a premium custom mandir. Decision-maker is overseas but the install happens at parents' home in India. Needs video consultations and remote approval workflow.

FAMILY BUSINESS OWNER FOR OFFICE: Buying a mandir for a family-run business / showroom / office reception. Larger scale, may include lighting and adjacent décor.

[Nilesh — confirm or expand based on actual past clients.]
brandDNA.anti_persona:
We DON'T serve:

— Customers shopping primarily on price, comparing us with ₹8,000–₹15,000 wood mandirs from local carpenters or online retail.

— Customers wanting a mandir delivered in 48 hours. Custom design takes weeks, not days.

— Customers refusing the design fee or design process — they want a quote first, design later. Our process is design-first, non-negotiable.

— Bulk wholesale to retailers who'd resell as their own — we're a direct-to-client studio.

It's better to politely decline these enquiries early than waste a designer's week on a deal that won't close on our terms.
brandDNA.audience_pain_points:
1. CONFUSION about budget. They want a mandir but have no idea if it costs ₹20k, ₹50k, or ₹2L. Quotes online vary wildly. They need a real conversation, not a price calculator.

2. FEAR of wrong design. Their reference images from Pinterest won't translate to their actual space dimensions, idol sizes, or wall layout. They're afraid of paying for something they'll regret.

3. CONFUSION about materials. Wood termites in 5 years? Marble too heavy for a flat? Corian — is that real? Each material has trade-offs the customer doesn't know to ask about.

4. VASTU UNCERTAINTY. Where exactly should it face? How high should idols be? Do they need a separate pooja room or is a wall-mount okay? Most retailers don't know — we do.

5. NO TRUSTED DESIGNER LOCALLY. Most carpenters in Pune do furniture, not mandirs. Real estate builders include generic units. Premium interior designers don't specialize in mandirs.
brandDNA.competitors: [Nilesh — please list the 3-5 specific competitors customers actually mention. Local carpenters? Specific Pune/Mumbai brands? Online players like Aarsun Woods? Direct Kishangarh marble suppliers? Mention by name so the team knows who to position against.]
brandDNA.competitive_position: Premium-tier, design-first, multi-material studio. NOT a retail showroom (we don't stock catalog units). NOT a carpenter (we design first, build second). NOT a marble supplier (we sell the finished, installed mandir, not just stone). We sit above the ₹10–30k mass market and below the ₹5L+ heritage-marble custom market.
brandDNA.usps:
1. 6+ MATERIALS UNDER ONE ROOF — Corian, Marble, Wood, HDHMR, Acrylic, Real Silver. Most competitors specialize in one. We can recommend the right material for YOUR home, not push the one we know.

2. DESIGN-FIRST PROCESS — Every project starts with a 2D design (2 options) followed by a 3D render. The client approves the design before any manufacturing. No surprises at delivery.

3. 6 BRANDABLE FRAMEWORK COLLECTIONS — Aaradhya Sinhasan™, Sthapna Series™, Pratibimb Panels™, Seva Cabinet™, Sanrakshak Series™, Divya Sthan™. Structured choices help the client decide.

4. PUNE-BASED WITH PAN-INDIA REACH — Free site visit in Pune; remote design + ship-install model for Mumbai, Nashik, Aurangabad, Kolhapur, and NRI projects.

5. PROFESSIONAL TEAM — Dedicated design head (Renuka Garud), in-house 3D visualizer (Shashank Punmiya), digital marketing, graphic design, and content. Not a one-man-shop.

[Nilesh — rank these or rewrite based on what wins most client conversations.]
brandDNA.never_compromise:
— We don't quote without designing first. Design fees are non-refundable and that's the rule. "Designing the mandir is the foremost and first exercise we follow to get on the same page with the client." — from our FAQ.

— We don't copy reference images blindly. "While reference images are helpful, they don't ensure a design that fits your specific needs and home aesthetics."

— We don't accept cancellations mid-order. Once design is approved and manufacturing has begun, the mandir is yours.

— We don't use particle board or termite-prone materials even if asked.

— We don't promise impossible timelines just to close a deal.

— We don't share clients' homes or photos without explicit permission.
brandDNA.proof_points:
— 300+ customers served across India
— 5.0 Google rating from verified families
— Projects ranging from ₹30,000 (essential wall-mount) to ₹25L+ (full pooja rooms)
— 6 framework collections covering Sinhasan, Back Panel, Wall/Floor Mount, Open Cabinet, Cabinet-with-Door, and Mandir Room
— Multi-material expertise: Corian, Marble, Wood, HDHMR, Acrylic, Real Silver
— Dedicated design team with in-house 2D + 3D visualization
— Active social presence: Facebook, Instagram (@make_my_mandir), Pinterest
— Featured on the website blog with educational content on material comparisons

[Nilesh — add specific numbers as you have them: years in business, % repeat clients, biggest single project value, awards/press.]
brandDNA.voice_attributes:
WARM — yes: "We'll guide you through the design step by step." — no: "As per the standard quotation process..."

DEVOTIONAL — yes: "A mandir is the most sacred element of your home." — no: "Religious furniture for your puja needs."

CONFIDENT (without arrogance) — yes: "The design comes first — that's how we work." — no: "Take it or leave it."

EDUCATIONAL — yes: "Wood looks beautiful but here's why Corian outlasts it in Pune's humidity." — no: "Buy our Corian, it's the best."

OCCASIONALLY HINGLISH (when emotional) — yes: "Jab ghar accha bana raha hai, toh mandir toh usse bhi accha hona chahiye!" — no: stiff English in every message.
brandDNA.tone_festival: More devotional, blessing-forward. Opens with a greeting (Jai Shri ___ / Subh ___). Centers the deity or festival meaning. Ends with a quiet wish, never a hard sell. Example opener: "On this auspicious Diwali, may every diya in your home glow brighter than ever."
brandDNA.tone_pricing:
Honest, design-first, never defensive. When asked "how much?":
1. Acknowledge the question without dodging
2. Explain that the cost depends on material + size + design — and that this is GOOD for the client
3. Offer the next step (free consultation / send floor plan via WhatsApp)
4. Mention design fees ARE non-refundable but they're redeemable against the order
5. End with a price range or starting point so they're not in the dark (e.g., "essential mandirs start at ₹30,000; premium pooja rooms can go up to ₹2L+")
brandDNA.tone_complaint:
Take ownership first. Never blame the client, the courier, the manufacturer.

First sentence template: "You're right — that shouldn't have happened. Here's what we're doing now to fix it..."

Then: clear next steps with timeline. Never "we'll look into it" — always "by tomorrow afternoon we'll have X resolved".

Escalate to founder Nilesh for anything involving installation defects, delivery damage, or a client questioning the brand's integrity.
brandDNA.tone_followup: Patient, never pushy. We follow up at 48 hours, then 7 days, then never again unless they re-engage. Each message offers value (a relevant blog post, a new framework, an open question) — never just "are you ready to buy?"
brandDNA.vocab_use:
USE: mandir, pooja, sacred space, devotion, divine, sthapna, sinhasan, divya sthan, custom, customised, design-first, craft, devotee, sanctum, blessed, family, home, journey.

USE COLLECTION NAMES with the ™: Aaradhya Sinhasan™, Sthapna Series™, Pratibimb Panels™, Seva Cabinet™, Sanrakshak Series™, Divya Sthan™.

USE Hinglish sparingly for emotional/festival posts (e.g., "Jab ghar accha bana raha hai...") — feels authentic, never forced.
brandDNA.vocab_avoid:
AVOID: cheap, lowest price, discount, sale, mass-produced, off-the-shelf, generic, religious furniture, wooden temple, carpentry, fabricated, factory-made, deal, bargain, offer ending soon.

AVOID Western fluff: "premium experience", "best in class", "redefining the industry", "unmatched quality".

AVOID generic salesy CTAs: "Buy now!", "Limited stock!". Use specific actions instead: "Send your floor plan on WhatsApp", "Book a free consultation", "DM us a photo of your space".
brandDNA.sentence_patterns:
PREFER short, declarative sentences for headlines and hooks. Save longer narrative sentences for the middle of captions/posts.

EXAMPLES we love:
- "A mandir is too sacred to come from a catalog."
- "Design first. Build second."
- "Where devotion meets design."

EXAMPLES we'd reject:
- "At MakeMyMandir, we believe in providing world-class customized mandir solutions to discerning customers." (corporate-speak, too long, says nothing)
- "Limited-time offer on our premium Corian range!" (salesy, not us)
brandDNA.color_primary:
IVORY WHITE — base/background. Hex: #F8F4EC (or similar warm cream).
WARM GOLD — primary accent (logos, highlights). Hex: #D4A574 / #C99A5B.
DEEP BROWN — text and bold elements. Hex: #2C1810 / #3D2A1F.

These form the trinity. Every post should feel within this palette.
brandDNA.color_secondary:
SOFT BEIGE — secondary background tone for cards and panels. Hex: #F0E6D2.
MAROON ACCENT — for festival posts, devotional emphasis. Hex: #8B3A1F (sparingly).

Avoid heavy use of secondary — they support, never lead.
brandDNA.color_dont:
NO bright primary blue, neon, or saturated tech-startup colors — they break the warm devotional mood.
NO pure white (#FFFFFF) — feels clinical; always use ivory/cream.
NO pure black (#000000) — too harsh; use deep brown instead.
NO cold greys — every neutral should be warm-tinted.
brandDNA.typography:
HEADLINES: Playfair Display (serif, elegant, traditional). Used for post headlines, page titles, hero text.

BODY: Lato or Open Sans (sans-serif, clean, readable). Used for body copy, captions, descriptions.

NEVER use: Comic Sans (obviously), generic Arial, decorative festival fonts that feel kitsch.

For Sanskrit/Hindi text, Mukta or Noto Sans Devanagari are appropriate.
brandDNA.logo_rules: [Nilesh — please add specifics: min logo size in posts, clear space around it, where it lives on posts (typically bottom-right watermark), allowed color variations (gold on dark? maroon on cream?), and what NOT to do.]
brandDNA.photography_style: REAL CLIENT HOMES preferred over studio shots. Soft warm natural daylight, OR warm LED accent lighting (3000K). Hero product centered or rule-of-thirds. Tight detail shots for material/carving close-ups; wide context shots for installation reveals. Brass diyas or marigolds in foreground for festival/devotional shots. People silhouettes acceptable from behind in respectful poses — never the family's faces unless they've explicitly agreed.
brandDNA.photography_donts:
NO stock photos.
NO harsh overhead lighting or flash.
NO cluttered backgrounds — declutter before shooting.
NO heavy filters that distort the actual mandir colors.
NO cold blue/white lighting.
NO faces of clients unless explicit written consent.
NO photos that look like real estate listings.
brandDNA.iconography:
Subtle Indian motifs as accents — never as the primary visual. Lotus, om, diya silhouettes, kalasha, swastika (where culturally appropriate and non-controversial for the audience). Line weight should be thin to medium, not heavy/blocky. Use in corners or as subtle backgrounds, not as headlines.

[Nilesh — confirm specific motif preferences and any to avoid.]
brandDNA.awareness_message:
First impression posts/ads should lead with the design-first promise. Examples:

— "A mandir is too sacred to come from a catalog."
— "We design your mandir in 2D and 3D — before you commit to anything."
— "Custom mandirs across 6 materials, designed for your home."

[Nilesh — confirm or refine. The key is to differentiate from generic furniture/mandir ads immediately.]
brandDNA.consideration_message:
Educational content. Material comparisons (Corian vs Wood vs Marble — pros, cons, longevity). Vastu guidance (placement, height, direction). Process explainers (what happens in the free consultation? what does 2D vs 3D design look like?). Behind-the-scenes content of the studio + team.

This is where the blog, Hooks & Scripts library, and Knowledge Base earn their value.
brandDNA.decision_message:
For clients on the fence, address the last hesitation. Common ones:

— "The design fees feel like a barrier." → Frame it as: it's redeemable against the order, and it's the difference between guessing and knowing what you'll get.

— "What if I don't like the design?" → That's exactly WHY we design first. If the design doesn't feel right, we revise. Order only proceeds after you approve.

— "Can you deliver outside Pune?" → Yes, with remote consultation + delivery + installation.

Close with the next concrete action: WhatsApp +91 7767962441 OR book a free consultation.
brandDNA.retention_message:
Post-installation:
1. Day-of: ensure smooth installation, take photos for portfolio (with permission), share Care & Maintenance guide.
2. Week 1: WhatsApp check-in: "How does it feel having the mandir in place?"
3. Month 1: Share the relevant material's specific care tips.
4. Festival times (Diwali, Akshaya Tritiya, Janmashtami, etc.): warm greeting, never a sales push.
5. Year 1: Anniversary message + offer to share their finished mandir on our social if they're willing.

[Nilesh — confirm exact cadence based on what's worked.]
brandDNA.referral_ask:
Best moment: 1-2 weeks AFTER installation, once they've used the mandir for daily pooja and the joy is fresh.

Ask: "Thank you for letting us be part of your home's most sacred corner. If you've enjoyed working with us, would you consider leaving a quick Google review? And if you know any family or friend looking for a custom mandir, we'd be honored if you'd mention us."

Never pushy. Never incentivized with discounts (cheapens the brand). The referral comes from genuine satisfaction, not transactional reward.
brandDNA.client_treatment:
— Always reply to a DM/WhatsApp within 4 working hours, even if just to say "We'll have a full reply by tomorrow morning."
— Free consultation is genuinely free — never bait-and-switch.
— Site visits: arrive 5 minutes early, remove shoes before entering pooja area, never photograph idols without permission, take measurements ourselves (never trust verbal dimensions).
— Every quote is preceded by a design — never quote off a 2-line WhatsApp brief.
— Be honest about timelines. If festival rush means 5 extra days, say so upfront.
— Never criticize a competitor by name. Educate; don't trash-talk.
brandDNA.team_culture: [Nilesh — please add. Some prompts: How do designers give each other feedback? When is something "ready" to send to client vs needs another pass? What's encouraged (curiosity, asking when unsure)? What's not tolerated (rudeness to clients, missed commitments without communication)?]
brandDNA.quality_bar:
FOR A POST: Has a clear hook. Caption is in our voice (not generic). Hashtags are right. Image/video is in our photography style. Brand DNA voice check passed. Approved by admin.

FOR A 2D DESIGN: 2 options offered to client. Proportions verified against client's space dimensions. Idol placement, framework choice, and material confirmed in writing.

FOR A 3D DESIGN: Rendered from at least 3 angles. Lighting represents the actual install lighting. Materials look photorealistic, not generic.

FOR A FINISHED MANDIR (before despatch): QC checklist — joins, finish, hardware, packaging. No mandir leaves the workshop with a defect we'd see in our own home.
brandDNA.escalation:
[Nilesh — please specify. Suggested starting points:

TEAM CAN DECIDE: minor design tweaks, content scheduling, replying to standard FAQs, choosing among approved templates.

MUST COME TO NILESH: pricing decisions, scope changes mid-project, refunds/complaints, partnership/dealer enquiries, brand voice questions, hires.]
